action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
I saw a giant candy box,
With sprinkles on the top.
My tummy said, "Oh no, no, no,
You're going to be too much!"
I took a bite, and it was sweet,
But it made me feel a bit...
Like I was eating a marshmallow,
That was too much for me!

SECTION 2
Now my tummy is a little round,
Like a balloon in the air.
It's full of sugar, it's full of joy,
But it's not a happy day.
I can't run fast, I can't jump high,
I'm just a little bit...
Like a cookie that's been baked too long,
And it's getting a bit sticky!

SECTION 3
My teeth are chattering, my eyes are red,
I can't even chew my bread.
The doctor said, "Don't eat the sweets,
Or you'll get a sweet tooth!"
So I'll go back to my lunch,
With a little bit of bread,
And maybe a glass of water,
To wash away the sweet!

SECTION 4
So next time you see a candy bar,
Don't be a little bit of a star.
Eat your veggies, eat your fruit,
And keep your tummy in the right.
Because a happy tummy is the best,
And a happy tummy is the best!
action.label: response
